name: Tensino Aragonese (Valle de Tena / Panticosa)

ancestors.notes: Tensino is a Central/Western High-Aragonese valley variety inheriting the whole Aragonese grapheme→IPA system; its distinctiveness is largely morpholexical (the ro/ra article, verb morphology) and only the article's weak-tap realisation is modelled.
Hispanic Vulgar Latin, inherited via Aragonese (an).
Basque/Aquitanian Pyrenean substrate, inherited via Aragonese.

notes: Tensino — the Aragonese of the Valle de Tena (Panticosa, Sallent de Gállego, Sandiniés; the Panticosa sub-variety is 'panticuto'), a Central/Western High-Aragonese variety (the reference grammar is Nagore Laín 1986, 'El aragonés de Panticosa. Gramática'). Its diagnostic feature is the POST-VOCALIC DEFINITE ARTICLE ro, ra, ros, ras (vs general Aragonese o, a, os, as / lo, la, los, las), also found in Ballibió, Sobrepuerto and Sobremonte. Vázquez Obrador (2021, RFRM 38:121-132, read directly) argues these derive from Latin ILLU / geminate -LL- by AUTOCHTHONOUS evolution (not Gascon borrowing): the proclitic atonic article's geminate lateral weakened to an apical-palatal fricative /ɽ/ and thence to the alveolar TAP /ɾ/ — a 'fonema líquido vibrante flojo'. This is the only phoneme-level point modelled here: the four article forms are given as word_exceptions realising ⟨r⟩ as the weak tap [ɾ] (not the word-initial trill [r] the base would otherwise assign). Tensino keeps the Western/Central profile inherited from an (F- preserved, /θ/ distinción, /ʎ/ for ⟨ll⟩, ⟨ch⟩=[tʃ]). FEATURES NOT PHONEME-MAPPABLE (documented, not modelled — phonemic spelling, lexical/morphological): retention of the cacuminal ⟨-ch-⟩ [tʃ] where general Aragonese has -ll- in a few words (estach, gricha); conservation of intervocalic voiceless -p-/-t-/-k- and participial -t- (words simply spelled with them); preterite in -ós (puyós, cantós); the genuinely Aragonese conditional in -arba/-erba/-irba (Nagore); pronominal-adverbial 'bi'. Diphthongisation Ĕ/Ŏ → ie/ue inherited (Várvaro, AFA XLVI-XLVII, p. 250: uá surfaces in the Valle de Tena documents). TIER RATIONALE: skeleton — Tensino's distinctiveness is overwhelmingly morpholexical (article, verb endings) rather than grapheme→IPA, so the spec is deliberately minimal; the article's tap realisation is the one modelled phonetic delta. PROVENANCE HONESTY: Vázquez Obrador (2021) and Várvaro (AFA) read directly; Nagore's Panticosa monograph consulted via secondary summaries only.

sources.author: Nagore Laín, F.
sources.title: El aragonés de Panticosa. Gramática
sources.notes: The reference grammar of the Tena-valley (Panticosa) Aragonese. Consulted via secondary summaries; primary text not directly read in this work.

sources.author: Vázquez Obrador, J.
sources.title: En torno al origen de los alomorfos (e)ro, (e)ra, (e)ros, (e)ras del artículo determinado aragonés
sources.notes: Read directly. Derives the ro/ra/ros/ras article from Latin ILLU/-LL- via retroflexion of the geminate liquid to a weak tap /ɾ/; localises it in the Tena valley.

sources.author: Várvaro, A.
sources.title: De la escritura al habla: la diptongación de O breve tónica en el Alto Aragón
sources.notes: Read directly. uá diphthong attested in the Valle de Tena documents (p. 250).
